title: Average (expected) comparison count of linear search
statement_informal: For a uniformly random present target, linear search performs on average (n+1)/2 comparisons. Proved exactly: the cost at 1-based position p is p, and the n positions sum to n(n+1)/2.

cost_model.notes: Cost at 1-based position p is exactly p comparisons (lemma lsearch_at). Averaged uniformly over the n positions gives (n+1)/2; the total 1+...+n = n(n+1)/2 is proved without division.

references: Verify-track twin of conjecture artifact tools/conjecture/results/linear-search.json (E[cost]=(n+1)/2, limit law Uniform).
Cormen, Leiserson, Rivest, Stein — Introduction to Algorithms, average-case analysis of linear search.
